lines: The chapel's own saint, carved in good stone long before any of the rest of this. Hands folded in prayer, robe to the floor, the work of someone who believed it mattered.

The head is gone, struck off at the neck and never set back. A wound-star has been cut into the breast, deep and recent. The quarantine wardens left the saints standing, the way the Remnant leaves its forms in place even where it has stopped meaning them. The Choir did not. They unmake the old holy things on purpose, to make room for the new.
Whatever this house was turned into after, it was built as a chapel first.
